subject: enable pooling and liveness in pdp-d
commit_message:
enable pooling and liveness in pdp-d

Change-Id: I4b29367fd419f4ae31cf455a21b8df80c231c3fa
Issue-ID: POLICY-795
Signed-off-by: Jorge Hernandez <jh1730@att.com>

Patch Set 1:

> (1 comment)

Right, but if the original was wrong (likely), then do we want to propagate it forward?

Patch Set 1:

At this point so late in this release, it's not a good idea to experiment, and deviate from existing pattern that has been proven it works.   Let's not delay this review to be merged please.

Patch Set 1:

I'll wait to see your patch tomorrow Mahendra, and run some tests.  Important thing, is that you allow for a higher number of replicas than the 2 initialization ones, when scaling that component: helm upgrade -i dev local/onap --namespace onap --set policy.pdp.replicaCount=3.   Otherwise I suggest to keep this change as it works.

So, I'll remove the pap.conf change, and retest with your changes applied.   Hopefully after I remove the pap.conf change, you can approve, and move on with the rest.

Patch Set 3:

Hello Jorge,

I have made the changes and now dynamic scale in/out should be able to generate the required pdp urls. I would like you to verify following at your end:

1. PAP is getting correct configurations. The format of the URLs.
2. Once an upgrade is performed, pap is restarted automatically to read the new configurations. Any changes in the configurations for pap will force k8s to restart the pap pod when helm upgrade is performed. 

Thx,
Mahendra

Patch Set 3: Code-Review+1

Thank you Mahendra. Folks let's try to get this tested today and pushed through in order to close up JIRA's and meet RC2.

Patch Set 3:

Mahendra, I'm very concerned with these additional capabilities added 1 day before release, just because of the cosmetics of the PAP_PDP_URL (which was following the original accepted one).   

We should have let through my review got as it was working, and put this on top, so it can be tested independently   

Again, the discrepancy on the PAP_PDP_URL to make it pretty, not sure if worth the risks at this point in time.   Again, this is better in a different patch or in Casablanca.

Not sure what you said about restarting the PAP, if configuration changes happen.  If that's the case, I don't want to restart the PAP if I grow one PDP-X, and trigger a chain reaction, that most likely won't work.   Not sure if you I may have misunderstood that, but I will be surprise if that brings the system clean, but again I may have misunderstood you.

Another issue, eyeballing the change in the PAP_PDP_URL will be different than the original one, so may need to modify the java code, to make it work.   Note that the PAP_PDP_URL should end with ../pdp/, and here will end up with the following: testpdp,alpha123,testpdp,alpha123, which may or may not work, but configuration wise is not correct.

I suggest pulling this into another review, and get mine through.  I'll test it independently today on top of mine, it it works will merge it, if not redone for Casablanca. 

I need guidance from you on the exact set of commands for the upgrade that you want to test.

Patch Set 3:

Jorge, I understand your concerns.
It was better way of doing it but its late.
But still you want to test it and give it a try (otherwise just remove these changes and add the pap.conf again as earlier for this release) then 

1. please run normal helm install commands to see if pap is being provided with correct configurations and its able to use the config:
 helm upgrade -i dev4 local/onap  --namespace onap4 -f onap/resources/environments/disable-allcharts.yaml --set policy.enabled=true --set global.pullPolicy=IfNotPresent
2. Then try to use helm upgrade with --set policy.pdp.replicaCount=4 to see pdp scales out and pap pod is restarted:
 helm upgrade -i dev4 local/onap  --namespace onap4 -f onap/resources/environments/disable-allcharts.yaml --set policy.enabled=true --set global.pullPolicy=IfNotPresent  --set policy.pdp.replicaCount=4 and check the pap again to see configurations are correctly mounted and pap is able to use them

Patch Set 3:

BTW the pap.conf you uploaded with additional pdp urls was having counter 0,1,3,4 which is not correct, it should be 0,1,2,3 for pdp statefulset names.

Patch Set 3:

Mahendra, I'll give it a try later and let you know ..

BTW, the counter "-3" it was a typo in my comments here, it actually generated a dev-pdp-2 (besides 0, 1).   I actually checked the wiki page I mentioned for which I captured the output and the new one was indeed "dev-pdp-2" (so just a typo), it doesn't skip numbers.

Patch Set 3:

Mahendra, I gave it a test with the last patch you added.   These are my observations:

1. The expanded configuration in the PAP has duplicated configuration credentials for the last PDP.   The software tolerates this, and there's no harm in functionality.

2. After modifying replica count per "helm upgrade" command discussed in comments to upgrade a PDP0X, the system became pretty messy.   PAP goes down, the 2 new PDP-Xs comes up first, after a while, PAP takes a bit to come back up, only 2 of the 4 PDP-Xs are detected in the PAP as part of the group.    The policies are in some PAPs but not in others, so going through the service gives inconsistent results.

Even if everything was to work fine, one major issue I find with this is forcing bringing down the PAP when growing a PDP-X instance when which is unnecessary, and causes service disruption, in UI, and other update functionality that requires group information.

I'm quitting for the day now.   Based on this, I think we should go to my first patch, with the change in the PAP_PDP_URL line to not skip the 2, which you were right on, when you pointed it out.  So if you agree if you could pull out the latest patch from the review, I would revert back to the original one tomorrow morning not skipping the 2.

Let me know how you guys want to move forward.

Patch Set 3:

Ok, I will pull out my changes and then you can upload your latest change to have pdp-2 in urls.

Patch Set 4: Code-Review+1

Folks, with it being RC2 today. Can we get this merged? I am confident that Jorge has done a very thorough job in testing the changes needed for the policy team to pass S3P functionality. Thanks!!
Change has been successfully merged by Mike Elliott

Patch Set 4:

Hey Mike, there was one mistake and it needed to be corrected by Jorge. Its a minor change. Will raise the patch again.

My bad, I should have corrected it earlier.

Thx,
Mahendra
